title: [9] Simplify MCP install — drop manual checksum-verify step (or automate it) + refresh install guide
why_we_need_this: The `mcp/README.md` release-binary install path currently makes users manually download `checksums.txt` and run `shasum -a 256 -c` before using the binary — friction the owner wants gone (2026-07-07). It's also inconsistent with the dashboard flow, which does NOT force users to manually verify checksums (the boot-time SHA check was removed in PR #45; the dashboard still publishes SHA256SUMS but requires no manual verification). Goal: the default MCP install flow should not require the user to fetch a checksum file and run a check — either remove that step (make verification optional) or automate it (an install helper that verifies transparently). The signed release artifacts stay exactly as they are; only the install UX/docs change.
acceptance_criteria:
- The default/happy-path install instructions in `mcp/README.md` must let a user download and run the release binary WITHOUT manually downloading `checksums.txt` or running `shasum -c`. Implement ONE approach:
  - (A — recommended, lowest effort) Remove the checksum step from the default flow; move both `shasum -c` and `cosign verify-blob` into an OPTIONAL \"Verify the download (recommended)\" subsection for the security-conscious. Keeps all artifacts, removes friction.
  - (B) Add an install helper (e.g. `mcp/install.sh` or a documented one-liner) that auto-detects OS/arch, downloads the asset + `checksums.txt`, verifies, extracts, and installs — so verification happens automatically and invisibly. If you use a `curl | sh` pattern, note its own supply-chain trade-off in the docs.
- Lead the guide with the `go install` path as the primary, inherently friction-free install (no checksum step at all).
- FIX the known `go install` version bug while editing the guide: it currently says `@mcp/v0.0.3`, which fails with \"disallowed version string\". The correct form for this submodule is `@v0.0.3` (Go maps the semver to the `mcp/vX.Y.Z` git tag itself). Add a one-line note explaining the tag-vs-go-install-version distinction. This bug is live in the published README and breaks every external `go install` user.
- DO NOT remove checksum generation or cosign signing from the release pipeline — `checksums.txt` + `.sig`/`.pem` must still be published (they back the Task #8 supply-chain story). This task changes install UX/docs only, not `mcp/.goreleaser.yaml`'s `checksum`/`signs` config.
- Note the trade-off explicitly in the PR body: dropping the default checksum step reduces the integrity guarantee cosign/checksums provide; the docs must still make verification easy (optional section, option A) or automatic (option B).
- After the change lands, run `update_project_context` so the mcp-server route reflects the simplified install flow.
- Git conventions: `docs:` prefix (or `feat:` if option B adds an install script), matching `docs/`- or `feat/`-prefixed branch, PR-based.
important_constraints:
- Install UX/docs only. Do NOT remove checksum/cosign artifact generation from the release; do NOT touch `mcp/.goreleaser.yaml`'s `checksum`/`signs` blocks, `dashboard/`, or `terraform/`.
- The rationale is consistency with the dashboard's no-forced-manual-verification flow — but the release still publishes signed checksums for anyone who wants to verify; keep verification documented/possible, just not mandatory on the happy path.
- Do not re-open the Task #8 packaging design beyond the install-flow docs.
- Do not edit anything under `project-context/` directly — Praxis-managed via `update_project_context` afterward.
- Files expected to change: `mcp/README.md` (+ optionally a new `mcp/install.sh` if option B is chosen). Nothing else.
